title: Getting Started with REDCapExploreR
text: Install development version GitHub: Load package installation:
code: devtools::install_github("CHOP-CGTInformatics/REDCapExploreR") library(REDCapExploreR)

title: Getting Started with REDCapExploreR
text: REDCapExploreR workflows need REDCap API endpoint API token access project reviewed. common setup store values environment variables read start analysis: examples assume redcap_uri token set. Avoid storing API tokens directly scripts, vignettes, version-controlled files.
code: redcap_uri <- Sys.getenv("REDCAP_URI") token <- Sys.getenv("REDCAP_TOKEN")

title: Getting Started with REDCapExploreR
text: Use get_record_status_data() retrieve plotting-friendly table summarizes REDCap form completion status record. pass result plot_record_status() create ggplot heat map similar REDCap Record Status Dashboard. package includes small synthetic dataset examples offline exploration. mock_record_status_data generated mock_redcap_project using internal record-status logic supports get_record_status_data().  larger projects, compact mode reduces x-axis text size, truncates long form labels, uses lighter tile borders. plot_record_status() returns ggplot object, can add ggplot2 layers customize labels display settings.
code: status_data <- get_record_status_data(   redcap_uri = redcap_uri,   token = token )  plot_record_status(status_data) mock_record_status_data #> # A tibble: 9 × 4 #>   record_id event_name     form_name                pct_complete #>   <fct>     <chr>          <fct>                           <dbl> #> 1 C001      baseline_arm_1 Baseline : Demographics         1     #> 2 C001      month_1_arm_1  Month 1 : Follow-up             1     #> 3 C001      month_1_arm_1  Month 1 : Adverse Events        1     #> 4 C002      baseline_arm_1 Baseline : Demographics         1     #> 5 C002      month_1_arm_1  Month 1 : Follow-up             0     #> 6 C002      month_1_arm_1  Month 1 : Adverse Events        0     #> 7 C0003     baseline_arm_1 Baseline : Demographics         0     #> 8 C0003     month_1_arm_1  Month 1 : Follow-up            NA     #> 9 C0003     month_1_arm_1  Month 1 : Adverse Events        0.667  plot_record_status(mock_record_status_data) plot_record_status(mock_record_status_data, mode = "compact")  plot_record_status(   mock_record_status_data,   mode = "compact",   form_label_max = 20 ) plot_record_status(mock_record_status_data) +   ggplot2::labs(title = "REDCap Record Status")

title: Getting Started with REDCapExploreR
text: Use build_codebook() pull REDCap project metadata return structured codebook fields, choices, forms, events, event-instrument mappings, repeating instrument settings, project-level summary metadata. Use mock_codebook inspect structure without API credentials. Use view_codebook() review codebook interactive HTML object one table per available section.
code: codebook <- build_codebook(   redcap_uri = redcap_uri,   token = token )  codebook$fields codebook$choices codebook$forms mock_codebook #> <REDCap codebook> #> Project: Mock REDCap Database #> Forms: 3 #> Fields: 11 #> Events: 2 #> Repeating enabled: TRUE  head(mock_codebook$fields) #> # A tibble: 6 × 19 #>   field_order form_order form_name  form_label field_name field_label field_type #>         <int>      <int> <chr>      <chr>      <chr>      <chr>       <chr>      #> 1           1          1 demograph… Demograph… record_id  Record ID   text       #> 2           2          1 demograph… Demograph… age        Age at enr… text       #> 3           3          1 demograph… Demograph… enrollmen… Enrollment… text       #> 4           4          1 demograph… Demograph… sex        Sex         radio      #> 5           5          2 follow_up  Follow-up  visit_date Visit date  text       #> 6           6          2 follow_up  Follow-up  response_… Response s… text       #> # ℹ 12 more variables: descriptive_field <lgl>, required_field <lgl>, #> #   identifier <lgl>, choice_count <int>, choices <chr>, validation <chr>, #> #   branching_logic <chr>, event_count <int>, event_names <chr>, #> #   repeating_status <chr>, field_note <chr>, matrix_group_name <chr> mock_codebook$project #> # A tibble: 1 × 29 #>   project_title  project_id field_count form_count event_count repeating_enabled #>   <chr>               <dbl>       <int>      <int>       <int> <lgl>             #> 1 Mock REDCap D…       1001          11          3           2 TRUE              #> # ℹ 23 more variables: creation_time <lgl>, production_time <lgl>, #> #   in_production <lgl>, project_language <chr>, purpose <lgl>, #> #   purpose_other <lgl>, project_notes <lgl>, custom_record_label <lgl>, #> #   secondary_unique_field <lgl>, is_longitudinal <lgl>, #> #   has_repeating_instruments_or_events <lgl>, surveys_enabled <lgl>, #> #   scheduling_enabled <lgl>, record_autonumbering_enabled <lgl>, #> #   randomization_enabled <lgl>, ddp_enabled <lgl>, project_irb_number <lgl>, … viewer <- view_codebook(codebook) viewer  htmltools::save_html(viewer, "codebook.html")

title: Getting Started with REDCapExploreR
text: Use build_quality_report() retrieve project records metadata, run general data quality checks, return findings, summaries, interpreted metadata. Use mock_quality_report see shape report output example findings. report can limited selected check groups focused review needed.
code: report <- build_quality_report(   redcap_uri = redcap_uri,   token = token )  report$findings report$summaries$project report$summaries$fields mock_quality_report #> <REDCap quality report> #> Records: 3 #> Fields: 11 #> Findings: 8  mock_quality_report$findings |>   dplyr::select(     check,     issue,     severity,     record_id,     form_name,     field_name,     message   ) #> # A tibble: 8 × 7 #>   check       issue              severity record_id form_name field_name message #>   <chr>       <chr>              <chr>    <chr>     <chr>     <chr>      <chr>   #> 1 metadata    high_risk_free_te… info     NA        follow_up visit_not… Free-t… #> 2 outliers    outside_validatio… warning  C002      demograp… age        Field … #> 3 outliers    future_date        info     C002      demograp… enrollmen… Date f… #> 4 operational incomplete_form_s… info     C0003     demograp… demograph… Comple… #> 5 operational incomplete_form_s… info     C002      follow_up follow_up… Comple… #> 6 operational incomplete_form_s… info     C002      adverse_… adverse_e… Comple… #> 7 operational incomplete_form_s… info     C0003     adverse_… adverse_e… Comple… #> 8 consistency checkbox_none_wit… warning  C002      follow_up symptoms   Checkb…  mock_quality_report$summaries$project #> # A tibble: 1 × 8 #>   record_count raw_row_count field_count form_count event_count #>          <int>         <int>       <int>      <int>       <int> #> 1            3            10          11          3           2 #> # ℹ 3 more variables: repeating_enabled <lgl>, missing_rate <dbl>, #> #   finding_count <int> report <- build_quality_report(   redcap_uri = redcap_uri,   token = token,   checks = c("missingness", "metadata") )

title: Getting Started with REDCapExploreR
text: pull_redcap_project() retrieves raw REDCap records project metadata used quality reporting workflow. can useful need normalized API pull custom exploration. top-level structure represented mock_redcap_project.
code: project <- pull_redcap_project(   redcap_uri = redcap_uri,   token = token )  names(project) names(mock_redcap_project) #> [1] "data"                  "metadata"              "events"                #> [4] "event_instruments"     "instruments"           "repeating_instruments" #> [7] "project_info"  mock_redcap_project$metadata #> # A tibble: 11 × 11 #>    field_name      form_name      field_type field_label  select_choices_or_ca…¹ #>    <chr>           <chr>          <chr>      <chr>        <chr>                  #>  1 record_id       demographics   text       Record ID    NA                     #>  2 age             demographics   text       Age at enro… NA                     #>  3 enrollment_date demographics   text       Enrollment … NA                     #>  4 sex             demographics   radio      Sex          1, Female | 2, Male |… #>  5 visit_date      follow_up      text       Visit date   NA                     #>  6 response_score  follow_up      text       Response sc… NA                     #>  7 visit_notes     follow_up      notes      Visit notes  NA                     #>  8 symptoms        follow_up      checkbox   Symptoms     none, None | fatigue,… #>  9 ae_term         adverse_events text       Adverse eve… NA                     #> 10 ae_grade        adverse_events text       Adverse eve… NA                     #> 11 ae_related      adverse_events radio      Related to … 1, Yes | 0, No         #> # ℹ abbreviated name: ¹​select_choices_or_calculations #> # ℹ 6 more variables: text_validation_type_or_show_slider_number <chr>, #> #   text_validation_min <dbl>, text_validation_max <dbl>, required_field <chr>, #> #   branching_logic <chr>, identifier <chr>

what: Exploratory Data Quality Tools for REDCap Projects
title: Exploratory Data Quality Tools for REDCap Projects
text: goal REDCapExploreR provide users tools perform exploratory data analysis quality assessments REDCap project data using REDCap API. repository early stages active development!

title: Exploratory Data Quality Tools for REDCap Projects
text: can install development version REDCapExploreR like :
code: devtools::install_github("CHOP-CGTInformatics/REDCapExploreR")

what: REDCapExploreR: Exploratory Data Quality Tools for REDCap Projects — REDCapExploreR-package
title: REDCapExploreR: Exploratory Data Quality Tools for REDCap Projects — REDCapExploreR-package
text: Provides tools explore REDCap projects, summarize project structure, flag common data quality issues data managers.

title: Build a REDCap codebook — build_codebook
text: build_codebook() pulls REDCap project metadata API returns structured, tidy codebook intended resemble information users see REDCap UI codebook remaining useful downstream R workflows.

title: Build a REDCap codebook — build_codebook
code: build_codebook(redcap_uri, token)

title: Build a REDCap codebook — build_codebook
text: redcap_uri REDCap API URI. token REDCap API token.

title: Build a REDCap codebook — build_codebook
text: redcap_codebook list elements: fields: one row per REDCap field display-oriented metadata, parsed choice summaries, validation, branching logic, event applicability, repeating status available. choices: one row per parsed choice option. forms: one row per instrument/form. events: one row per REDCap event project longitudinal. event_instruments: one row per event/form mapping available. repeating: repeating instrument/event configuration available. project: project-level summary metadata.

title: Build a REDCap codebook — build_codebook
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ codebook <- build_codebook(   redcap_uri = Sys.getenv("REDCAP_URI"),   token = Sys.getenv("REDCAP_TOKEN") )  codebook$fields codebook$choices } # }

what: Build a REDCap data quality report — build_quality_report
title: Build a REDCap data quality report — build_quality_report
text: build_quality_report() pulls REDCap project API, applies general data quality heuristics, returns findings, summaries, interpreted metadata.

title: Build a REDCap data quality report — build_quality_report
code: build_quality_report(   redcap_uri,   token,   checks = c("missingness", "metadata", "outliers", "operational", "consistency"),   sparse_threshold = 0.95,   outlier_iqr_multiplier = 3,   progress = c("auto", "none", "show") )

title: Build a REDCap data quality report — build_quality_report
text: redcap_uri REDCap API URI. token REDCap API token. checks Character vector check groups run. sparse_threshold Missingness threshold used flag unexpectedly sparse fields. outlier_iqr_multiplier Multiplier used IQR-based numeric outlier detection. progress Progress display mode. Use "auto" show progress interactive sessions, "none" suppress progress, "show" force progress output.

title: Build a REDCap data quality report — build_quality_report
text: list findings, summaries, metadata elements. findings includes record, form, event, repeat instrument, repeat instance context available REDCap export. summaries$project$raw_row_count equals nrow() records returned REDCap API. summaries$project$field_count count distinct standardized metadata field names. summaries$fields$missing_rate field-level fraction missing among applicable rows. summaries$forms$missing_rate summaries$project$missing_rate weighted fractions missing across applicable field-row cells. Applicability accounts form availability, event-form mapping, repeating instrument structure, branching logic. Missingness assessed raw REDCap form status column <form_name>_complete 1/Unverified 2/Complete. Status 0/Incomplete handled operational checks contribute missingness, even REDCap exports checkbox choices 0.

title: Build a REDCap data quality report — build_quality_report
text: Current findings$issue values findings$check: missingness required_field_missing unexpected_sparse_field metadata duplicate_field_label missing_field_label missing_choice_definition orphaned_branching_reference high_risk_free_text outliers outside_validation_range numeric_iqr_outlier future_date operational incomplete_form_status consistency checkbox_none_with_other

title: Build a REDCap data quality report — build_quality_report
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ report <- build_quality_report(   redcap_uri = Sys.getenv("REDCAP_URI"),   token = Sys.getenv("REDCAP_TOKEN") ) } # }

what: Get Record Status Dashboard data — get_record_status_data
title: Get Record Status Dashboard data — get_record_status_data
text: get_record_status_data() retrieves REDCap records project structure API, returns plotting-friendly tile table similar high-level REDCap Record Status Dashboard.

title: Get Record Status Dashboard data — get_record_status_data
code: get_record_status_data(redcap_uri, token)

title: Get Record Status Dashboard data — get_record_status_data
text: redcap_uri REDCap API URI. token REDCap API token.

title: Get Record Status Dashboard data — get_record_status_data
text: tibble one row per dashboard tile columns: project record ID field, form_name, pct_complete. Longitudinal projects also include event_name, REDCap unique event name.

title: Get Record Status Dashboard data — get_record_status_data
text: REDCap stores form status one *_complete field per instrument. function normalizes status values joins record/form grid built API records, metadata, instrument metadata, event metadata, event-instrument mapping. classic projects, output contains one row per record instrument. longitudinal projects, output contains one row per observed record/event/instrument combination enabled event-instrument mapping. Observed record/event combinations taken API rows, including rows exist repeating instrument exported. Repeating instrument instances summarized tile rather returned separate rows. tile's pct_complete value proportion relevant instances marked complete. Non-repeating instruments evaluated non-repeating rows avoid structural blanks repeating instruments. Completion values returned proportions 1 means relevant statuses complete, 0 means relevant statuses complete, NA means completion status available tile. record identifier column uses REDCap project's record ID field name, project infseq_id record ID field returns infseq_id column. form_name factor ordered REDCap event instrument order. longitudinal projects, form_name uses "Event Label : Form Label" labels available.

title: Get Record Status Dashboard data — get_record_status_data
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ get_record_status_data(   redcap_uri = Sys.getenv("REDCAP_URI"),   token = Sys.getenv("REDCAP_TOKEN") ) } # }

title: Mock REDCap project — mock_redcap_project

code: mock_redcap_project

title: Mock REDCap project — mock_redcap_project
text: list REDCap API-like data frames: data Synthetic REDCap records. metadata Synthetic REDCap data dictionary metadata. events Synthetic longitudinal event metadata. event_instruments Synthetic event-instrument mapping. instruments Synthetic instrument labels. repeating_instruments Synthetic repeating instrument settings. project_info Synthetic project-level metadata.

what: Plot Record Status Dashboard data — plot_record_status
title: Plot Record Status Dashboard data — plot_record_status
text: plot_record_status() creates ggplot heat map data returned get_record_status_data(). plot designed resemble REDCap Record Status Dashboard remaining standard ggplot object users can extend additional ggplot2 layers.

title: Plot Record Status Dashboard data — plot_record_status
code: plot_record_status(   data,   record_id_field = NULL,   mode = c("standard", "compact"),   low_color = "#D73027",   mid_color = "#FEE08B",   high_color = "#1A9850",   missing_color = "grey95",   tile_color = "white",   tile_linewidth = NULL,   x_label = "REDCap Form",   y_label = "Record ID",   fill_label = "Completion Status",   rotate_x_text = TRUE,   x_text_size = NULL,   y_text_size = NULL,   x_tick_every = NULL,   y_tick_every = NULL,   show_y_title = NULL,   form_label_max = NULL )

title: Plot Record Status Dashboard data — plot_record_status
text: data dataframe returned get_record_status_data(). record_id_field Optional string naming record ID column. default, function infers column form_name, event_name, pct_complete. mode Plot display mode. Use "standard" default dashboard view "compact" smaller x-axis text, thinner tile borders, truncated long form labels. Compact mode preserves default y-axis labels title unless overridden directly. low_color Color pct_complete = 0. mid_color Color pct_complete = 0.5. high_color Color pct_complete = 1. missing_color Fill color missing pct_complete values. tile_color Border color dashboard tile. tile_linewidth Border width dashboard tile. NULL, value chosen mode. x_label X-axis label. y_label Y-axis label. fill_label Fill legend label. rotate_x_text Whether rotate form labels top axis. x_text_size, y_text_size Optional axis text sizes. x_tick_every, y_tick_every Optional positive integers showing every nth x- y-axis tick label. NULL, axis thinned. arguments work standard compact modes. show_y_title Whether show y-axis title. form_label_max Optional positive integer truncating long form labels. NULL, compact mode truncates labels 35 characters standard mode leaves labels unchanged.

title: Plot Record Status Dashboard data — plot_record_status
text: ggplot object.

title: Plot Record Status Dashboard data — plot_record_status
text: function expects one row per dashboard tile form_name column pct_complete column. record ID column inferred remaining data columns possible. longitudinal output, event_name ignored event labels already included form_name. pct_complete range 0 1, 1 complete 0 incomplete unverified. Missing values shown missing_color.

title: Plot Record Status Dashboard data — plot_record_status
code: plot_record_status(mock_record_status_data)   plot_record_status(mock_record_status_data, mode = "compact")   if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data <- get_record_status_data(   redcap_uri = Sys.getenv("REDCAP_URI"),   token = Sys.getenv("REDCAP_TOKEN") )  plot_record_status(data) } # }

what: View a REDCap codebook as interactive HTML tables — view_codebook
title: View a REDCap codebook as interactive HTML tables — view_codebook
text: view_codebook() turns redcap_codebook object build_codebook() static HTML viewer tab-style section navigation one interactive table per codebook section. returned object prints RStudio/Posit Viewer can saved HTML file htmltools::save_html(). Event, event-instrument, repeating-structure sections omitted API tables empty.

title: View a REDCap codebook as interactive HTML tables — view_codebook
code: view_codebook(codebook, page_length = 25)

title: View a REDCap codebook as interactive HTML tables — view_codebook
text: codebook redcap_codebook object returned build_codebook(). page_length Number rows show per interactive table page.

title: View a REDCap codebook as interactive HTML tables — view_codebook
text: htmltools browsable HTML object containing interactive DT tables.

title: View a REDCap codebook as interactive HTML tables — view_codebook
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ codebook <- build_codebook(   redcap_uri = Sys.getenv("REDCAP_URI"),   token = Sys.getenv("REDCAP_TOKEN") )  viewer <- view_codebook(codebook) htmltools::save_html(viewer, "codebook.html") } # }
